subject: foxes
one_liner: A video platform for foxes. Upload bin footage, get sorted by scream, and earn money you are structurally unable to collect.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan, faux-corporate, a sincere founder pitch that hasn't quite landed. Treats foxes as a serious, underserved creator demographic and never acknowledges the obvious problem. No winking. No earnest startup uplift.

hero.sub: Skulk is home to 4 million nocturnal creators uploading footage of overturned bins, territorial screaming, and brief eye contact at the treeline. Built mobile-first for animals without thumbs.

features.title: Motion-Triggered Uploads
features.blurb: Skulk starts recording the moment a fox enters frame and publishes before the fox has left the garden. Foxes cannot operate a record button. We have classified this as a feature and moved on.

features.title: Scream Recognition
features.blurb: Our audio model sorts all 28 documented fox screams by emotional register, from territorial to inconsolable, and tags every clip automatically. Confidence stays high as long as no fox is actually screaming nearby.

features.title: Den-Side Monetization
features.blurb: Earn 0.003 cents per thousand views, paid in a currency foxes cannot hold to an account foxes cannot open. The payout button is large, red, and satisfying to press.

pricing_tiers.name: Kit
perks: A watermark shaped like a paw

pricing_tiers.name: Skulk Pro
perks: We remove the paw and add a larger paw

pricing_tiers.name: Apex
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the fox running a small media estate.
perks: A manager who is also a fox

testimonials.quote: I uploaded forty seconds of a bin and woke up with a following. I have not seen the bin since.
testimonials.name: Reynard Tillery
testimonials.title: Red Fox, 4.1M subscribers, mostly bins

testimonials.quote: The tagger filed my best work under inconsolable. It was meant as a greeting. We are in talks.
testimonials.name: Margot Underbrush
testimonials.title: Urban Vixen and Lifestyle Creator, North London

testimonials.quote: Nobody asked whether foxes want this. They built it anyway. The retention curve is, admittedly, remarkable.
testimonials.name: Dr. Pell Hawthorne
testimonials.title: Wildlife Ethologist Who Was Not Consulted

demo_screen_concept: Skulk Studio, the creator analytics dashboard for one fox's channel. A frosted-glass sidebar (Overview / Content / Audience) over a twilight gradient, a top bar with the channel name and a Nocturnal Mode toggle. Overview shows glass stat cards (screams logged, bins overturned, watch time after midnight, subscribers), a seven-night activity bar chart in pure CSS, and a retention note. Content is a filterable list of uploaded clips with mock thumbnails, view counts, scream-tag chips, a publish/unlist toggle per row, and a filter to show only clips recorded after 2am. Audience shows where viewers den, as labeled bars. Interactive: tab switching, the toggle reskins the panels, the after-2am filter hides rows, and clicking a clip opens a glass modal with fake playback stats.

design_direction.style: Nocturnal glassmorphism streaming app. A deep twilight-aubergine night sky as the ground, with a soft low moon and two restrained, heavily blurred aurora washes (one fox-orange, one moonlight-blue) sitting far behind the content. Foreground UI is frosted translucent glass: panels with backdrop-blur, a 1px top highlight border, soft layered shadows, and rounded 18-22px corners. Content density is kept high so it reads as a real streaming product, not a wallpaper. Fox-orange is the single action color (play buttons, live dots, the primary CTA); moonlight-blue is a cool secondary used for verified badges and links. Crisp geometric display type against a clean humanist body. No purple-on-purple AI blob soup, no neon glow on text, no skeuomorphic gloss.

fonts.body: "Plus Jakarta Sans", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif

design_direction.archetype: Streaming / media product landing (glassy now-playing hero, three feature glass cards, three testimonials, three glass pricing tiers, demo CTA, footer)
design_direction.motion_vocab: Glass cards lift a few pixels on hover, the live dot and play button pulse softly, aurora washes drift very slowly behind the glass, the now-playing card has a thin progress bar that creeps. All motion is gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Full-bleed twilight gradient with a soft moon top-right and two blurred aurora washes far behind. Left column: eyebrow chip, large display headline, deadpan sub, fox-orange primary CTA plus a ghost secondary. Right column: a floating frosted now-playing card showing a fox clip thumbnail, title, channel row with a moonlight verified badge, play control, scream-tag chips, and a creeping progress bar. A thin row of live stats sits under the hero.
